get.description: Returns patients specifically waiting for a doctor at a given facility.

current_patient.description: If the doctor has an active session

post.description: The 'Next Patient' button logic. Automatically pulls the highest priority patient from the waiting list.

409.description: Doctor already has an active consultation

post.description: Transitions the encounter to 'IN_PROGRESS'. If no ID is provided, it attempts auto-assignment.

get.description: Deep-dive into patient history: Past Vitals, Active Meds, and ICD10 History.

post.description: Saves SOAP notes and executes the final clinical decision (Discharge, Admit, Refer, etc.)
Bypass the main decision engine to send a patient to another department immediately.

get.description: Returns a simplified list of the most recently registered patients for the reception dashboard.
Fetches standard system lists like Genders and Marital Statuses for building registration forms.
Search for patients using Name, MRN, or Phone Number scoped by Facility.

post.summary: Find existing patient or create new one
Find/Create patient and start encounter (Reception Flow)
post.description: The primary entry point for reception. It deduplicates the patient and puts them directly into the Triage queue.

message.example: New patient registered and sent to triage

422.description: Patient already has an active encounter

get.description: Returns a paginated list of patients waiting in the Triage station.
Aggregated view of alerts, past vitals, active diagnoses, and medications for clinical decision support.

post.description: Updates the queue status to 'CALLED' and assigns the current staff member to the patient. This action triggers the notification for the patient to proceed to the triage room.

message.example: Patient called to triage. Please proceed.
Patient is already being handled by another staff member.

post.description: Marks the patient as 'In Service'. This timestamp is used to calculate the 'Call-to-Entry' time (walking time from bench to desk).

call_to_entry_seconds.description: Time in seconds from Call to Start

message.example: Triage completed. Patient moved to doctor queue.

post.description: Used when a patient's condition deteriorates in the waiting area. Teleports the patient from Triage queue to the very top of the Doctor's queue with an EMERGENCY priority flag.

message.example: Emergency bypass successful. Patient at top of doctor queue.

post.description: Allows nurses to initiate lab/rad orders before doctor consultation.

200.description: Orders created and routed to department worklists

subjective.example: Patient reports persistent headache for 3 days.

assessment.example: Likely tension headache, rule out malaria.

chief_complaint.example: Persistent dry cough and chest pain

last_meal_time.description: Accepts Y-m-d H:i:s or Unix Timestamp

vitals.description: Vital signs. Use 'BP' as 'SYS/DIA' string; others use database codes.

department_id.description: Optional: Nurse override for destination clinic (e.g., Dental, ENT)
